Parameter: Cost per facility birth (bundled PPH program)
Source / notes: I'm assuming $2/birth. Includes: HSC drug ($0.31), prorated TXA drug ($0.18), training increment ($0.70), supply chain ($0.35), monitoring ($0.35). Highly uncertain — at $1/birth CE roughly doubles; at $3/birth CE drops to ~2x. AMPLI-PPHI cost data (expected mid-2026) would resolve this.

Parameter: Maternal lives saved per 100,000 facility births with HSC
Source / notes: India modeling study (2023), cited in PMC review. Compares HSC to settings without reliable uterotonic (cold-chain failure). I could not access the original study.
Modeling study, not direct RCT in the target setting. Based on CHAMPION trial (HSC non-inferior to oxytocin) plus assumptions about cold-chain failure rates.
India model applied to broader LMIC settings. Cold-chain reliability varies across settings.

Parameter: Mortality rate among PPH cases (standard care)
Source / notes: WOMAN trial placebo arm: 191/9,985 = 1.91%

Parameter: Relative risk reduction from TXA (1 - RR)
Source / notes: WOMAN trial: RR 0.81 (95% CI 0.65-1.00) for death from bleeding. Using WOMAN trial as primary source for mortality. 2024 Lancet IPD meta-analysis (OR 0.77 for life-threatening bleeding composite) is confirmatory.
Large, high-quality multi-country RCT (WOMAN trial). Confirmed by 2024 IPD meta-analysis. Small discount for field vs. trial conditions.
WOMAN trial included LMIC sites, but TXA requires rapid PPH diagnosis and IV/IM administration within 3 hours. Effectiveness in field conditions may be lower than trial.

Parameter: Adjusted deaths averted per PPH case treated
Source / notes: Calculation: CFR x RRR x IV x EV
Calculation
Calculation. Slight overcount: HSC prevents some PPH, reducing TXA treatment pool. Effect is small (~10%).

Parameter: UoV per maternal death averted (GW moral weights)
Source / notes: GiveWell standard moral weight for LMIC adult death. Maternal deaths are women aged 15-49.
Calculation

I'm assuming +75%. PPH causes severe anemia, need for blood transfusion, surgical interventions (hysterectomy, laparotomy), and long-term disability. For every PPH death, many women survive with significant morbidity. Rough guess.

Parameter: Treatment costs averted uplift (% of mortality UoV)
Source / notes: I'm assuming +25%. Blood transfusions are scarce and expensive in LMICs; surgical interventions carry high cost. Rough guess.
Calculation: mortality UoV x (1 + morbidity + treatment)

Best-guess estimate. Highly sensitive to cost/birth: at $1/birth -> ~6.5x; at $3/birth -> ~2.2x. Confidence: Low.

NEJM 2023: bundled PPH early detection + treatment reduced composite outcome (severe PPH, laparotomy, or death) by 60% (RR 0.40, 95% CI 0.32-0.50). Supports the bundled approach modeled above but uses a different intervention package.
WHO 2025: misoprostol is 'last resort' when no injectable options are available
AMPLI-PPHI ($26M, 6 countries, 2022-2026) is generating cost-effectiveness evidence. Results expected by mid-2026.
